title: Build side-by-side comparison matrix for the two agent-docs plan sets
description: Create a structured comparison of the vision-oriented plan set and the spec-oriented plan set across definition, scope, mandatory core, contracts, policy, CLI, reports, and rollout shape.

title: Extract canonical claims from the vision-first plan set
description: Pull out the authoritative statements from the vision-authority-driven plan so they can be compared without prose duplication.

title: Extract canonical claims from the spec-first plan set
description: Pull out the authoritative statements from the minimal-canonical-truth spec so they can be compared without prose duplication.

title: Map overlaps contradictions and unique strengths across both plan sets
description: Identify where the two plan sets agree, where they differ, and which unique ideas should survive into a merged direction.

title: Define decision criteria for keep merge or supersede outcomes
description: Write the criteria that will be used later to decide whether one plan set wins, both are merged, or both are rewritten.

title: Define the small mandatory anti-mess core for agent-docs
description: Lock what must always be in core regardless of profile or pack, including canonical truth, generation, checks, and gates.

title: Freeze the canonical source artifact taxonomy
description: Choose the reduced canonical source kinds and distinguish them cleanly from generated or derived human-facing views.

title: Define the native contract semantic core and stable IR schema
description: Turn the RFC contract direction into a concrete IR schema with stable nodes, traits, serialization, and diffability rules.

title: Define importer and emitter capability maps plus semantic loss reporting
description: Specify how imports and outputs declare supported capability coverage and how semantic loss is surfaced instead of being hidden.

title: Define compatibility classes breaking-change rules and impact analysis model
description: Classify additive, compatible, risky, and breaking changes for contracts and generated consumers.

title: Define glossary alias and forbidden-alias governance rules
description: Specify canonical term storage, aliasing behavior, forbidden alias enforcement, and cross-artifact terminology validation.

title: Define the native contract and policy CLI command surface
description: Lock the first-class command set for contract import emit diff break-check impact docs doctor and related policy verification commands.

title: Define repo policy engine rules severities and enforcement points
description: Specify which rules warn, which fail, and where enforcement runs across hooks, local checks, and CI.

title: Define profiles packs and optional capability boundaries
description: Separate the minimal required system from optional packs for governance, verification, operations, and language outputs.

title: Define package split across core contracts policy generators and profiles
description: Choose the internal package layout and module boundaries for the implementation work.

title: Define migration path from external contract shell-outs to native contract subsystem
description: Plan the incremental migration away from command wrappers so the repo keeps working while contracts become first-class.

title: Define generated output inventory and freshness guarantees
description: List exactly which docs bindings validators reports and summaries are generated and how freshness is checked.

title: Define runtime validator fixture and contract-test strategy
description: Specify the runtime validation story so generated outputs go beyond static types and fail early at boundaries.

title: Define evaluation metrics for AI-harnessed development improvements
description: Choose the measurable signals for duplication drift compatibility safety review quality and autonomous-task success.

title: Draft merged RFC skeleton for the chosen or synthesized direction
description: Create the implementation-oriented RFC outline once the comparison, core choices, and constraints are settled.
